steps.title: Express the obtuse angle
steps.content:
The acute angle is $(10x-470)^\circ$. Since each acute angle and adjacent obtuse angle are supplementary, an obtuse angle has measure:
$$180-(10x-470)=650-10x$$

steps.title: Match the given sum form
steps.content:
The sum of one acute angle and three obtuse angles is:
$$(10x-470)+3(650-10x)=10x-470+1950-30x=-20x+1480$$

steps.title: Compare constants
steps.content: The problem says this sum is $(-20x+w)^\circ$. Since the simplified expression is $-20x+1480$, the constant term is $w=1480$. The answer is 1480.
